channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: SEC slaps Elon Musk with a lawsuit. Here’s why
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has sued billionaire Elon Musk, saying he failed to disclose his ownership of Twitter stock in a timely manner in early 2022, before buying the social media site.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Tech CEOs are just postponing their Trump 2.0 reckoning
item.description: In Silicon Valley, a million bucks doesn’t exactly count as vast riches. Still, it’s a nice, round figure pregnant with symbolic value. And so it meant something when major tech companies and their leaders lined up to donate that amount to Donald Trump’s fund for his second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Congressman Ro Khanna calls for ‘full and transparent’ investigation into death of OpenAI whistleblower Suchir Balaji
item.description: "I am heartbroken by your loss. Given your very serious concerns about foul play, I do believe that there should be a full and transparent investigation into the death by the FBI or appropriate agency," the South Bay congressman wrote in a social media post Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jakob Rodgers, The Mercury News

item.title: Biden's big semiconductor law will ramp up US chip production -- but at a high cost, report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sweeping 2022 law, touted by President Joe Biden as a way to revive U.S. manufacturing of semiconductors and reduce the country’s reliance on foreign-made computer chips, will “sharply increase production’’ of semiconductors in the United States.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with explosion of online porn
item.description: The Supreme Court weighed whether an explosion in online pornography requires repudiating the court's precedents concerning sexual content as the justices Wednesday heard arguments in a challenge to Texas’s age-verification law for porn websites.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk accused by SEC of cheating Twitter investors out of millions
item.description: The agency’s complaint accuses the billionaire of failing to promptly report that he had amassed more than 5% of the social-media platform’s stock in early 2022.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: The death of DEI in tech
item.description: Save me from rich, white men who insist they and their kind are being discriminated against. Tech entrepreneur Elon Musk, majority owner (not the founder) of SpaceX, Tesla, and numerous other leading companies, insists that “DEI [Diversity, equity, and inclusion] is just another word for racism.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Female Ties at Heart of Celtic Social Networks, Study Finds
item.description: Genetic evidence from a late Iron Age cemetery in southwest England reveals that women were closely related, while unrelated men tended to come into the community after marriage.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: These top online AI courses can help advance your career
item.description: Demand for generative AI (genAI) courses is surging, passing all other tech skills courses and spanning fields from data science to cybersecurity, project management, and marketing.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What Elon Musk’s growing political clout could mean for 2025
item.description: Elon Musk, who once largely eschewed politics, now has a political clout of his own to throw around as President-elect Trump heads back to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Reimagine application modernisation with the power of generative AI
item.description: In a global economy where innovators increasingly win big, too many enterprises are stymied by legacy application systems. As a consequence, these businesses experience increased operational costs and find it difficult to scale or integrate modern technologies.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: One of iPhone’s best features is finally going to Disney World and Disneyland
item.description: Disney is now adding support for one of the iPhone’s best features to its mobile ordering system for the Disneyland and My Disney Experience” for Disney World iOS apps.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: California pulls back EPA requests for approval to phase out diesel-powered trains and big rigs
item.description: California has withdrawn requests for the EPA to give the state authority to enforce rules limiting emissions from diesel-powered trains and big rigs.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Austin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg will cohost reception with Republican billionaires for Trump inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is cohosting a reception with billionaire Republican donors next week for Donald Trump’s inauguration, the latest sign of the Facebook founder's embrace of the president-elect.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Court lets Tennessee porn site age verification law take effect as Texas law goes to Supreme Court
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A panel of appeals judges has ruled that Tennessee can begin enforcing a law that requires pornographic websites to verify their visitors’ age as the First Amendment debate rises to the U.S. Supreme Court this week for oral arguments over a similar Texas law.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inside the DHS Science and Technology Directorate’s search for better wireless communications
item.description: The Homeland Security Department's Science and Technology Directorate has an ongoing research program for the development of cellular. The post Inside the DHS Science and Technology Directorate’s search for better wireless communications first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Biden signs ambitious order to bolster energy resources for AI data centers
item.description: President Joe Biden has signed an ambitious executive order on artificial intelligence that seeks to ensure the infrastructure needed for advanced AI operations like data centers can be built quickly and at scale in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The journey towards a knowledge graph for generative AI
item.description: How does the journey to a knowledge graph start with unstructured data—such as text, images, and other media? The evolution of web search engines offers an instructive example, showing how knowledge can be extracted from unstructured sources and refined over time into a structured, interconnected gr
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft component retirement frenzy geared to product simplification: Analyst
item.description: A rash of planned feature and application retirements within the Microsoft 365 environment is the result of Microsoft wanting to centralize, modernize, operationalize customer spend, and ultimately economize by simplifying product offerings where it can, an industry analyst said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Brazil law restricts use of smartphones in elementary and high schools
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil's President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Monday signed a bill restricting the use of smartphones at school, following a global trend for such limitations. The move will impact students at elementary and high schools across the South American nation starting in February.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and DIARLEI RODRIGUES, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 5-minute cleanup for your phone and computer
item.description: Technology expert Kim Komando shares her easy tips on how to declutter your phones, computers and devices from the files you no longer need.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Britain's leader unveils AI strategy, including vow to rip up red tape hindering AI projects
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government pledged on Monday to rip up the red tape hindering construction of data centers that underpin artificial intelligence while promoting its “pro-innovation” approach to regulating the technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nvidia year in review
item.description: 2024 was Nvidia’s year. Its command of mindshare and market share was unequaled among tech vendors. Here’s a recap of some of the key events of 2024 that highlight just how powerful the world’s most dominant chip player is.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Researchers build a bridge from C to Rust and memory safety
item.description: Memory errors such as out-of-bounds reads and writes and use-after-free bugs have plagued applications for decades, causing problems ranging from minor execution glitches to global security nightmares.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: The survey also found that 77 percent of employers said they will adopt the strategy of “reskilling and upskilling.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Working with minimal APIs in .NET
item.description: If you’re building cloud-native applications with microservices, they need to be small. You don’t want code that comes with enough dependencies and required libraries to turn something that should be able to scale in milliseconds into megabytes of code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4
